hard.next_step: 6/26/26 MQ Opportunity re-appeared in my funnel unexpectedly and without notice. No progress on deal since I moved it to SAM rep Bertrand Lesne, who subsequently transferred it to Monika Mutscher. Stage was changed to shortlisted by Pankaj Sahu on 6/9/26 before being transferred back to me on 6/17. I reverted stage back to formal eval and have reached out to Kelley Benson via email to re-connect. 3/4/26 MQ Scheduled deep dive discovery call with Kelly for 3/20 @ 2:00. Trying to pull Imon Ahmed into call. 1/21/26 MQ Kelly requested we push our discovery call to later in February. 12/5/25 MQ Followed up with Kelly to schedule discovery and demo. Asked her to pull in Imon Ahmed, Director of Global P2P. 11/18/25 MQ Kelly will share presentation with internal stakeholders. Current Zycus CLM customer.
hard.ais_why: Nov 18 2025 intro call: Kelley Benson's team uses Zycus for indirect CLM and Coupa for purchase requests while exploring AI-driven end-to-end S2P; specific interest in Merlin Intake, Anna (autonomous negotiation) and iRisk, AI rated 'Very Critical'. Existing Zycus CLM footprint reduces friction; score tips to 8 on the AI appetite.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live Zycus CLM account exploring S2P, but the only real sales touch is the single 18 Nov 2025 intro call where Kelley Benson disclaimed decision authority ('I'm not the decision maker'); a scheduled 20 Mar 2026 discovery was cancelled and nothing ran for seven months. Marc's 26 Jun 2026 re-connect email drew a same-day reply from Kelley redirecting to global P2P owner Imon Ahmed, who has never been engaged. Deal is alive but stalled: no discovery held, no economic buyer engaged for the S2P scope, no champion.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 18 Nov 2025; close date slipped 30 Jan 2026 to 30 Sep 2026 (day 1), then to 30 Nov 2026 on 26 Jun 2026. Stage churned Qualified to Formal Evaluation (4 Mar, amount 200k to 300k) to Shortlisted (9 Jun, Pankaj Sahu) then reverted to Formal Evaluation (26 Jun) and to Qualified (30 Jun). Currently in Qualified 6 days (30 Jun to 6 Jul); 147 days to close. Only one genuine sales call in ~230 days of deal age; last two-way buyer touch 26 Jun 2026 (10 days ago) is a redirect, not progress. A full enterprise S2P motion (discovery, EB, demos, RFP, shortlist, workshops, commercials, negotiation, InfoSec, contracting) in 147 days from a cold intro is not credible.
evidence: Rep-initiated re-connect email 26 Jun 2026; Kelley Benson replied same day: 'You really need to engage Imon Ahmed, as Zycus is a global offering from CHEP, so the relationship sits with her' (source: Task 26 Jun 2026)

Single sales call 18 Nov 2025 (32-minute intro with Kelley Benson); Kelley said current tools 'work pretty well', exploration is to 'broaden awareness', and 'I'm not the decision maker' (source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025)
Discovery call scheduled for 20 Mar 2026 was cancelled; 21 Jan 2026 Next Step: 'Kelly requested we push our discovery call to later in February' (source: Next_Step_History__c)
No economic buyer engaged for the S2P scope and no champion Contact Role; Kelley (the SF Decision Maker) disclaimed authority (source: SF OpportunityContactRole + Avoma 18 Nov 2025)

CHEP is a live Zycus CLM (iContract) customer with active premium support and weekly delivery connects (source: Avoma CHEP-Zycus Weekly Connect 24 Nov / 4 Dec 2025; Avoma intro call 18 Nov 2025 'we use Zycus as our CLM for our indirect procurement')

metrics.narrative: Kelley Benson articulated qualitative pain on the 18 November 2025 intro call: CHEP's indirect procurement is fragmented, with Coupa handling purchase requests and Zycus CLM handling contracts, and she is exploring AI-driven end-to-end S2P to 'broaden awareness' since taking the role in July. She engaged on Merlin Intake (Teams-based conversational intake), Anna (autonomous negotiation) and tail-end spend consolidation, but no hard numbers, savings target, cycle-time, FTE or maverick-spend figure was stated. Value is directional, not quantified. The move is to run the long-delayed discovery with the global P2P owner and quantify manual effort and tail spend into a measurable ROI case (source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025 Kelley Benson).

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged for the S2P scope. Kelley Benson, the SF primary Contact Role (Decision Maker), explicitly disclaimed authority on 18 Nov 2025 ('I'm not the decision maker') and on 26 Jun 2026 redirected Marc to Imon Ahmed, described in Marc's Next Step as 'Director of Global P2P' and by Kelley as the global system owner where 'the relationship sits'. Imon Ahmed is therefore the most likely decision/budget authority for a global S2P investment, but she has never been contacted despite being flagged in the 5 Dec 2025 and 4 Mar 2026 Next Steps. Because CHEP is already a live Zycus CLM customer, account-level access exists; the specific gap is engaging Imon for the S2P expansion. The move is to secure a discovery call with Imon and Kelley together (source: Task 26 Jun 2026 Kelley Benson email; SF Next_Step__c 5 Dec 2025 / 4 Mar 2026).

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are early and inferred from the 18 Nov 2025 call rather than a documented evaluation. Kelley signalled interest in AI-driven, Teams-integrated conversational intake (Merlin Intake), autonomous sourcing/negotiation (Anna), ongoing supplier risk monitoring (iRisk), and spend consolidation on tail-end spend, and she probed whether Merlin requires the Zycus CLM and whether it works with other CLMs. These map to a product-fit story Zycus is strong on, but no formal BRD, RFP, or scored criteria set exists yet. Confirming the real evaluation criteria is dependent on running the discovery with the global P2P owner (source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025 Kelley Benson).

decision_process.narrative: The decision process for the S2P expansion is unknown. The only sales call was a 32-minute intro; the deeper-dive discovery scheduled for 20 Mar 2026 was cancelled and never rescheduled, and the deal churned owners three times (Marc to Bertrand Lesne 19 Mar, to Monika Mutscher 22 May, back to Marc 17 Jun) and stages (Qualified/Formal Eval/Shortlisted/back to Qualified) with no buyer-driven progress. Kelley's 26 Jun redirect indicates the real process sits globally with Imon Ahmed, not with the Americas indirect team. The near-term move is to map who owns budget, approval and timing for a global S2P purchase via the discovery with Imon (source: OpportunityFieldHistory; Task 26 Jun 2026).

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have started on this expansion (stage is Qualified, forecast Pipeline). However, as a new-business expansion into an existing Zycus customer, paper would be lighter than a first-time full-suite deal: an Order Form / SOW against the existing commercial relationship rather than a fresh MSA, and CHEP already runs Zycus under premium support with an established procurement channel (Christina Yates, Procurement Analyst). Visibility here depends on reaching commercials, which is several milestones away. What creates visibility is completing discovery and confirming the global buying/approval chain (source: Avoma weekly connects 24 Nov / 4 Dec 2025 showing live CLM support relationship).

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed but qualitative. On 18 Nov 2025 Kelley Benson described a fragmented indirect procurement stack (Coupa for purchase requests plus Zycus CLM for contracts) and interest in removing manual effort through conversational intake and autonomous negotiation, but she also said the current tools 'work pretty well', which caps urgency. The pain owner is Kelley (Head of Indirect Source to Pay, in role since July 2025), with the global scope sitting under Imon Ahmed. No compelling event or forcing function (contract expiry, mandate, budget cycle) has been surfaced, which is why status-quo inertia is the leading threat (source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025 Kelley Benson).

champion.narrative: There is no champion. Kelley Benson, the closest contact, disclaimed decision authority on 18 Nov 2025 ('I'm not the decision maker', 'maybe other people that may be better to have that conversation'), committed to socialize the presentation internally, then went quiet for months and on 26 Jun 2026 handed the relationship off to Imon Ahmed. That is a coach/influencer at best, not an advocate driving the deal, and no other buyer joined a sales call. Christina Yates appears only on existing-CLM support calls, not on the S2P opportunity. Building a champion, most likely Imon Ahmed as the global owner, is the prerequisite before any commercial step (source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025 Kelley Benson; Task 26 Jun 2026).

competition.narrative: The primary competitor is the status quo / do-nothing: Kelley said the existing Zycus CLM plus Coupa P2P stack 'works pretty well' and the exercise is to 'broaden awareness', with no compelling event, so the biggest risk is that CHEP keeps its fragmented setup and never buys. Coupa is the incumbent for purchase requests and catalog purchasing (office supplies, MRO, Amazon) with neutral sentiment; there is no active Coupa-versus-Zycus bake-off for S2P. Our wedge is that Zycus already owns the indirect CLM, so an organically built, Teams-integrated, AI-driven end-to-end S2P (Merlin Intake, Anna, iRisk) unifies a stack that is split across two vendors today (source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025 Kelley Benson).

deal_movement.summary: The deal reopened after a long dormancy: a single 18 Nov 2025 intro call, a cancelled 20 Mar 2026 discovery, three owner reassignments and repeated stage/close-date churn, then Marc's 26 Jun 2026 re-connect email and Kelley's same-day reply redirecting to Imon Ahmed. Last real buyer touch 26 Jun 2026; no substantive progress since Nov 2025.

items.change: Marc emailed Kelley to re-connect; Kelley replied same day directing him to engage Imon Ahmed, the global P2P owner
Stage reverted to Qualified and close date slipped 30 Sep 2026 to 30 Nov 2026; forecast reset Best Case to Pipeline
Opportunity transferred back to Marc Quessenberry after routing through Bertrand Lesne and Monika Mutscher
Scheduled deep-dive discovery call with Kelley for 20 Mar 2026 was cancelled
Amount raised 200k to 300k; stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation

competitive_position.summary: Status quo / do-nothing is the single strongest current threat: on 18 Nov 2025 Kelley Benson said the existing Zycus CLM plus Coupa P2P stack 'works pretty well' and the effort is exploratory, with no compelling event to force a change. Coupa is the incumbent for purchase requests / catalog buying, neutral sentiment, and is NOT in an active S2P bake-off against Zycus. There is no third-party rival named in any source. We win by consolidating a stack we already half-own (Zycus CLM) into an AI-driven end-to-end S2P.

competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin Intake plus Anna as unifying intake-to-outcome across the same catalogs Coupa handles, leveraging the existing Zycus CLM data Coupa cannot touch.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong product-fit interest but no committed evaluation. Kelley engaged on AI-driven intake, autonomous negotiation and supplier risk on 18 Nov 2025; adoption and decision ownership are exposed because she is not the decision maker and the global owner is unengaged.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage expansion into a live Zycus account: the win path is reaching the global S2P owner, converting the existing CLM footprint into a wedge, and building urgency against status-quo inertia.
flags: Multi-thread to Imon Ahmed now: Kelley (a self-disclaimed non-decision-maker) said the global S2P relationship sits with Imon, so single-threading to Kelley is the top risk.
Turn the existing Zycus CLM footprint into the expansion wedge: CHEP already runs iContract on premium support, so lead with unifying the split Coupa+Zycus stack, not a cold new-vendor pitch.
Build a quantified business case to beat do-nothing: convert 'works pretty well' into measured manual-effort and tail-spend leakage tied to a global rollout date.

critical_signals.text: Imon Ahmed, the global P2P owner where the S2P relationship sits, was surfaced by Kelley on 26 June 2026 but has never been contacted.
The only genuine two-way touch since the 18 November 2025 intro call is Kelley's 26 June email redirecting us to Imon rather than advancing herself.
The real threat is inertia: Kelley said the existing Zycus CLM plus Coupa stack works pretty well, with no compelling event to force a decision.
At 300k with no economic buyer engaged and only qualitative pain, the value case cannot yet be justified to whoever owns the global budget.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Kelley Benson disclaimed decision authority on 18 Nov 2025, made commitments (share the deck, schedule discovery) she never fulfilled, went quiet for months, then handed the relationship to Imon Ahmed on 26 Jun 2026. No advocacy, no internal driving, no multi-thread. The nearest path to a champion is Imon Ahmed as the global P2P owner, who has not yet been engaged.
champion_strength.source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025 Kelley Benson; Task 26 Jun 2026; SF OpportunityContactRole

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI story landed well on the single call: Kelley engaged specifically on Merlin Intake, Anna and iRisk and asked follow-up questions on autonomous negotiation and tail-spend consolidation. The gap is not positioning but reach: the AI narrative has only been shown to a non-decision-maker and needs to be carried to Imon Ahmed and the global P2P team.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 8.0 / Strong with AI rated 'Very Critical', driven by real buyer questions on Merlin Intake, Anna (autonomous negotiation) and iRisk on 18 Nov 2025. The calls corroborate genuine AI curiosity but temper the buying intent: Kelley framed it as exploratory ('broaden awareness') and said current tools work pretty well, so appetite is real but not yet urgent.

items.detail: Single-thread to Kelley Benson, who disclaimed decision authority; the actual global S2P owner (Imon Ahmed) is unengaged.
No compelling event or forcing function; buyer says the current fragmented stack 'works pretty well', so status-quo inertia is the main loss risk.
No quantified value case and no budget owner confirmed for a global 300k S2P investment.

confidence_signals.summary: Thin evidence base: one genuine sales call in ~230 days, a cancelled discovery, and a single recent buyer email. Engagement reopened on 26 Jun but is a redirect, not momentum.
items: Only one matched sales call (18 Nov 2025); no discovery or demo ever held
Buyer redirect to a never-engaged global owner (Imon Ahmed) on 26 Jun 2026
Repeated stage and close-date churn with no buyer-driven progression

expansion_context.prior_opp: CHEP existing Zycus CLM / iContract deployment (indirect procurement, The Americas)
expansion_context.note: CHEP is a live Zycus CLM customer on premium support with active weekly delivery connects, so account-level access is inherited; do not flag 'no executive access'. The gap is expansion-specific: engaging the global P2P owner Imon Ahmed for the S2P scope.

scope_change.detail: Amount raised from 200k to 300k on 4 Mar 2026 with a full-suite product set (iSource, iSupplier, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA); scope has not narrowed.

customer_preference.evidence: Kelley Benson on 18 Nov 2025: current Zycus CLM + Coupa tools 'work pretty well', exploration is to 'broaden awareness', and 'I'm not the decision maker' — neutral, exploratory, no Zycus-specific commitment.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (fragmented indirect stack, manual effort, tail-end spend) articulated 18 Nov 2025; no ROI, savings, cycle-time or FTE numbers stated.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: CHEP is a live Zycus CLM account exploring S2P, but the only real sales touch is a single 18 Nov 2025 intro call where Kelley Benson disclaimed decision authority; on 26 Jun 2026 she redirected us to the never-engaged global P2P owner Imon Ahmed, so the one thing between us and progress is reaching Imon and running an actual discovery.

win_position.text: We hold a genuine expansion wedge: CHEP already runs Zycus CLM (iContract) on premium support, and on 18 Nov 2025 Kelley Benson engaged specifically on Merlin Intake, Anna and iRisk with AI rated 'Very Critical'.

The buyer is not leaning in: Kelley said the current Coupa-plus-Zycus stack 'works pretty well' and the effort is exploratory, so status-quo inertia, not a rival, is the biggest loss risk (Avoma, 18 Nov 2025).
There is no economic buyer or champion engaged for the S2P scope: Kelley disclaimed authority and handed us to Imon Ahmed on 26 Jun 2026, who has never been contacted.

deal_momentum.text: Only one genuine sales call in roughly seven months; the 20 Mar 2026 deep-dive discovery was cancelled and never rebooked (Next_Step_History__c).
The deal churned through three owners and back-and-forth stage/close-date changes with no buyer-driven progression, ending back in Qualified on 30 Jun 2026.
A live thread did reopen: Marc's 26 Jun 2026 re-connect email drew a same-day reply from Kelley, giving a warm re-entry point to the global owner.

customer_commitment.text: Kelley made and dropped commitments (socialize the deck, schedule discovery) and named herself a non-decision-maker, so there is no committed internal owner.
No concrete dates, no demo request follow-through, and no commercial conversation; the customer has not pulled the process forward.

deal_risk.text: Single-thread to a self-disclaimed non-decision-maker while the actual global S2P owner (Imon Ahmed) is unengaged.
No compelling event and no quantified value case for a 300k global investment; the deal can quietly die to do-nothing.

champion_strength.evidence: Kelley disclaimed authority and redirected to an unengaged owner; no advocacy (Avoma 18 Nov 2025; Task 26 Jun 2026).

customer_preference.evidence: 'works pretty well', exploratory only, no Zycus-specific lean (Avoma 18 Nov 2025).

momentum.evidence: one call in seven months, discovery cancelled, owner/stage churn (OpportunityFieldHistory).
